description: Add --json/--csv flags to all commands with TTY auto-detection for pipe-friendly output

userStories.title: TTY detection helper in output.rs
userStories.description: Add a `is_tty() -> bool` function that returns true when stdout is an interactive terminal. Use `std::io::IsTerminal` (stable since Rust 1.70) on `std::io::stdout()`. This becomes the single source of truth for whether to emit ANSI color or machine-readable output.
acceptanceCriteria: Function `pub fn is_tty() -> bool` exists in `src/output.rs`
Uses `std::io::IsTerminal::is_terminal(&std::io::stdout())`
No external crates added — std only
Unit test: cannot test real TTY in CI, but function must compile and be callable
Doc comment explains: returns false when stdout is a pipe, file redirect, or non-terminal

userStories.title: Add `--json` and `--csv` shorthand flags to Today/Week/Month
userStories.description: Alongside the existing `--format` flag on Today, Week, and Month commands, add `--json` (bool flag) and `--csv` (bool flag) as convenience aliases. Priority: `--json` > `--csv` > `--format`. If none are set and stdout is not a TTY, auto-detect: default to Json. Implement the resolution in `main.rs` via a helper `fn resolve_format(format: OutputFormat, json: bool, csv: bool) -> OutputFormat`.
acceptanceCriteria: `--json` flag added to Today, Week, Month in `cli.rs` (bool, default false)
`--csv` flag added to Today, Week, Month in `cli.rs` (bool, default false)
`resolve_format(format, json, csv) -> OutputFormat` in `main.rs` or `output.rs`
Resolution order: json flag → Json; csv flag → Csv; else use provided format value
`--json` and `--csv` cannot both be set — clap `conflicts_with` enforces this
`--json` conflicts_with `--format` is NOT enforced (--json simply wins; existing --format usage is unbroken)
All three query commands (Today/Week/Month) call resolve_format before passing to run_query
Integration test: `blackbox today --json` exits 0 and stdout is valid JSON
Integration test: `blackbox today --csv` exits 0 and stdout contains CSV header row

userStories.title: TTY auto-detection: pipe → JSON by default
userStories.description: When stdout is not a TTY (piped or redirected) AND neither --json, --csv, nor --format were explicitly passed, `resolve_format` should return `OutputFormat::Json`. Explicit flags always take precedence. The default_value on --format remains `pretty` for CLI help display purposes, but `resolve_format` overrides it when not in a TTY.
acceptanceCriteria: resolve_format: if !is_tty() && format == OutputFormat::Pretty && !json && !csv → return OutputFormat::Json
Explicit `--format pretty` in a non-TTY still emits pretty output (user opt-in respected)
Explicit `--format json` in a TTY still emits JSON (user opt-in respected)
In a TTY with no flags: OutputFormat::Pretty (unchanged behavior)
Test helper or note: TTY state is not mockable in assert_cmd tests; document that the auto-detect path is covered by manual testing or a unit test on resolve_format with a fake is_tty param
Unit test for resolve_format covering all 6 meaningful input combinations

userStories.title: Strip ANSI codes when stdout is not a TTY
userStories.description: The `render_summary_to_string` function in `output.rs` uses the `colored` crate which respects `NO_COLOR` and `TERM=dumb` env vars, but does not automatically strip colors when piped. Add a call to `colored::control::set_override(false)` when `!is_tty()` so ANSI codes never leak into piped output. This must be called before any rendering, ideally once at startup in `main.rs`.
acceptanceCriteria: `colored::control::set_override(false)` called when `!is_tty()` in main.rs before command dispatch
Existing `render_summary_to_string` output in non-TTY contains no ANSI escape sequences (\x1b[)
In TTY mode, colors are unaffected
Integration test: pipe `blackbox today --format pretty` output and verify no \x1b[ bytes present
Note: `colored` crate already supports `set_override` — no new dependency needed

userStories.title: Add `--json` flag to Standup command
userStories.description: The `standup` command currently only outputs plain text. Add `--json` and `--csv` flags (same pattern as Today/Week/Month). When --json: reuse `render_json` with the appropriate ActivitySummary. When --csv: reuse `render_csv`. TTY auto-detection also applies.
acceptanceCriteria: `--json` and `--csv` flags added to `Commands::Standup` in cli.rs
Standup command in main.rs calls resolve_format before output selection
When format is Json: `println!("{}", render_json(&summary))`
When format is Csv: `println!("{}", render_csv(&summary))`
When format is Pretty: existing render_standup behavior unchanged
Integration test: `blackbox standup --json` exits 0 and stdout is valid JSON

userStories.title: Document JSON output schema in --help
userStories.description: Update the `--json` flag help text to describe the top-level JSON shape so users know what to expect without reading source. Specifically: `{period_label, total_commits, total_reviews, total_repos, total_estimated_minutes, total_ai_session_minutes, repos: [{repo_name, repo_path, commits, branches, estimated_minutes, events, pr_info?, reviews, ai_sessions}]}`.
acceptanceCriteria: --json arg in cli.rs has a doc comment (/// string) describing output shape
`blackbox today --help` shows the schema hint
No code logic changes — doc only
cargo doc builds without warnings

userStories.title: Update README.md and AGENTS.md
userStories.description: Update project documentation to reflect new feature. README.md: add new command to Commands table if applicable, update feature descriptions. AGENTS.md: add new source files to Architecture tree, update Key Patterns if new patterns introduced.
acceptanceCriteria: README.md Commands table includes new command (if a CLI command was added)
README.md describes the new feature/capability
AGENTS.md Architecture src/ tree lists any new .rs files
AGENTS.md Key Dependencies updated if new crates added
userStories.notes: This must be the LAST story executed. Reference README.md and AGENTS.md in project root.
